text: least one hundred and eighty-five years; Lara could be gone in an instant. That ephemerality leant her unique beauty . Was that the purpose of life? Was it simply a transient state between creation and destruction, birth and death, existence and nonexistence? A thing made more precious by its very frailty, made more miraculous because it had survived overwhelming odds, a thing that defied the universe by simply being ? “My thoughts follow strange paths when I’m with you , Lara .” “What do you mean?” she asked, wiping moisture from her eyes. Her voice was raw. “ Like what ?” He pressed his palm to her back more firmly, just to feel the way her skin yielded to him. When he lifted his hand, her flesh would revert to its prior state . Would her inner fire rekindle, given enough time, or would she never be the same again ? She shifted her head to look into his optics. Her eyes — their blue made more vibrant by the red surrounding them — brimmed with tears, which rolled off her face to land on his arm. Though he had no data to support it, the sky had to have been the same color, long ago. But there was more depth in her gaze than in all the sky on the clearest day . “Like what it means to be alive .” A shuddering release of breath. “What do you think it means?” She rubbed her cheek against his arm, hiding most of her face from him again . “I’m not sure, yet. But I think I get a little closer, every moment I spend with you .” Lara was quiet for a long while, though her breathing remained uneven, and more moisture trickled onto Ronin’s skin. “I used to think it was just about surviving,” she said, after several minutes. “That’s all life was. Figure out how to trade enough stuff to eat, so I had enough energy to do it all over the next day .” She shook her head against him, gently, stray strands of hair falling over her face. “But that’s wrong. It’s not about surviving. We all do that, people and bots, but that’s not living . It’s…it’s about what you experience in that time, you know? About the joy you find,” she sniffled and rubbed her palms over her eyes again, “no matter what shit you go through, no matter how hard it gets . “We had that. It wasn’t enough, but me and Tabitha had it. And now…now it’s gone .” Her body shook with silent sobs. Ronin held her, saying nothing as she cried. Everything will be all right rose to the top of his list of responses. He dismissed it. He couldn’t say it with any certainty, and she deserved better than a lie . “It’s not gone,” he finally said. Softly, he tapped her temple. “All you had with her will always be right here, and it will always be yours .” She clung to him, her tears flowing freely, and he pressed his cheek to her hair, rubbing his hand along her back. Eventually, she calmed . Lara’s gaze met his optics, and, though the flesh around her eyes was puffy and red, they were dry. A faint smile blossomed on her kiss-swollen lips, only to wilt a second later. “I…didn’t know it would be like that .” “Didn’t know what would be like that?” There were too many variables, this time. Too many possibilities, too many chances for miscommunication . “Sex.” The little line appeared between her brows. “I expected pain, and I thought, with you, I could deal with it. That I owed it to you to take it, because I was using you to… forget .” “You don’t owe me anything.” Did she really think he’d ever demand such a price from her? “You did…enjoy our coupling, didn’t you ?” Her cheeks darkened . “ Yes .” Ronin found unexpected satisfaction in her response. She hadn’t often been happy since he’d met her, had only displayed small glimpses of joy, and knowing that he had given her one of those moments — despite the circumstances — pleased him . “Why did you think there’d be pain ?” “Because…that’s all it was before.” She averted her gaze again, her blush fading rapidly . He brushed stray locks of her hair behind her ear and gently guided her to look at him. “Tell me , Lara .” She pressed her lips together and swallowed. Finally, her tongue slipped out to wet her lips, and she spoke. “I knew it felt okay with hands and mouths. I messed around with that to earn a bite to eat, here and there. But Tabitha…she gave it all.” Her gaze settled on his chest, but her eyes were glossy and unfocused. “She let men and bots use her, fucked them however they wanted it. They paid her in credits. And, no matter how little I managed to scrape together to contribute, she always made sure I had food to eat. She was never selfish. She... loved me . “It ate at me most days, knowing what she was doing to keep us fed. She’d sacrificed so much. Nothing I did ever felt like enough. She deserved more from me, you know? Especially with all she did. So I…I went back to Kitty’s, determined to prove I wasn’t useless. That I could pull my weight and provide for her .” “Back? Were you a dancer there before ?” “Yeah. I tried, because I really wanted to help more. I liked dancing, so how hard could it be? Lasted a couple weeks, and then I walked out. Nobody kept their hands to themselves, and they didn’t give a shit about the human girls getting felt up .” God dammit, I said I don’t do that anymore ! The words she’d spoken that first night suddenly made sense. He should have known, with the way
